{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents provide evidence that CMV is a common infection post solid organ transplantation. Document [22] mentions that CMV infection and disease are important causes of morbidity and mortality in transplant recipients. It also states that CMV infection occurs in up to 50% of high-risk SOT patients, typically following organ transplantation from a CMV-seropositive donor to a CMV-seronegative recipient. No document provides similar evidence for HSV, EBV, or HPV as the most common infection post solid organ transplantation.", "answer_choice": "A"}
